outlets.name: CNN
outlets.headline: US conducts self-defense strikes in Iran; oil rebounds as ceasefire stress rises on Day 88
outlets.summary: Brent crude rebounded to $98.11/bbl from overnight Memorial Day low of $96.31 as CENTCOM self-defense strikes on IRGC mine-laying boats near Bandar Abbas added a geopolitical risk premium. Doha MOU talks continue. Austria nuclear report adds uncertainty to deal timeline.

outlets.name: NBC News
outlets.headline: US and Iran officials signal progress in negotiations, fragile ceasefire remains in force
outlets.summary: Trump Truth Social ultimatum: deal 'will either be great and meaningful, or there will be no deal.' Araghchi in Doha admits uranium deadlock. Pezeshkian: Iran will not surrender to pressure.

outlets.name: CBS News
outlets.headline: Live: Iran war — US peace talks and Strait of Hormuz deal 'still a work in progress'
outlets.summary: Iran delegation in Doha for Qatar-mediated talks. Araghchi: 'Iran never wanted nuclear weapons' but uranium is a deadlock 'postponed to later.' IDF strikes Tyre wounding 37 after Hezbollah drone attack.

outlets.name: Fox News
outlets.headline: Austrian intel report: Iran has missiles 'ready to carry nuclear warheads' — Tehran calls it 'fake'
outlets.summary: Austria's DSN annual intelligence report concludes Iran's nuclear weapons program is 'well-advanced' and Iranian ballistic missiles are 'ready to carry nuclear warheads.' Iran's Foreign Ministry called the report 'fake information.' Report directly contradicts US DNI Gabbard's March 18 testimony.

outlets.name: RedState
outlets.headline: Hegseth's West Point Speech: 'This Is the Way' — Army Hits 2026 Recruiting Goals Four Months Early
outlets.summary: Hegseth at West Point: 'Diversity is not our strength. Unity is our strength.' Army hit 2026 recruiting goals four months early. Speech praised by conservative media while Iran deal negotiations proceed.

outlets.name: The Hill
outlets.headline: Rubio warns of 'another way' if Iran talks fail — military option remains on the table
outlets.summary: Rubio in New Delhi issues the clearest US military threat since ceasefire. 'Another way' interpreted as resumed airstrikes. Republican hawks Wicker and Cruz remain opposed to any deal that defers nuclear issues.

outlets.name: Al Jazeera
outlets.headline: Day 88: Iran delegation still in Doha after CENTCOM strikes; oil rebounds; Austria nuclear report
outlets.summary: Iran's Doha delegation remains in Qatar for MOU talks despite CENTCOM self-defense strikes on IRGC mine-laying boats near Bandar Abbas overnight. Brent rebounded to $98.11 from $96.31 Memorial Day low. Austrian intelligence says Iran nuclear weapons program 'well-advanced.' Lebanon front quiet after Day 87 IDF 70+ site strikes.

outlets.name: Reuters
outlets.headline: Iran FM Araghchi admits uranium 'deadlock' with US as Doha talks begin
outlets.summary: Araghchi: uranium issue 'postponed to later stages.' Trump ultimatum and Rubio threat frame the negotiations as binary. Brent crude fell to $100.21 on deal optimism, down from $103.54 Thursday.

outlets.name: Bloomberg
outlets.headline: Oil Drops Below $101 as Trump, Rubio Push for Fast Iran Deal
outlets.summary: Brent crude fell to $100.21/bbl as Trump and Rubio both framed the deal as imminent but with consequences if it fails. WTI at $92.07. A deal could push Brent to $82-90 range, analysts say.

outlets.name: Times of Israel
outlets.headline: Soldier killed by Hezbollah drone; Netanyahu orders IDF to 'intensify blows' — Day 87
outlets.summary: Sgt. Nehoray Leizer (19) killed near Debel, Bint Jbeil — 11th IDF death since Lebanon ceasefire. Hezbollah drone hits Metula home. IDF strikes 25+ targets in Beqaa Valley and south Lebanon. Lebanese media: 4 killed, 3 wounded in Kfar Roumman, Kafr Qana, Nabatieh.

outlets.name: Jerusalem Post
outlets.headline: Israel 'very unhappy' with emerging Iran deal — officials say Hormuz is Iran's new nuclear weapon
outlets.summary: A senior Israeli official: deal is 'bad because it signals Iran possesses a weapon no less effective than a nuclear one' — the Strait of Hormuz. Netanyahu relying on direct Trump calls rather than envoy Witkoff.

outlets.name: Haaretz
outlets.headline: 'Fake Info': Iran protests Austrian intel report accusing Tehran of pursuing atom bomb
outlets.summary: Iran's Foreign Ministry slammed Austria's DSN annual intelligence report as 'fake information' after it concluded Iran's nuclear weapons program is 'well-advanced' and missiles are 'ready to carry nuclear warheads.' Israeli officials note the Austrian assessment aligns with Israeli intelligence.

outlets.name: Press TV
outlets.headline: Iran embassy in India rejects Rubio's accusation that Tehran 'taking energy market hostage'
outlets.summary: Iran's Delhi embassy called Rubio's characterization 'a clear attempt to distort the realities of the region.' Iran delegation in Doha. Iran VP Aref: Iran will not give up Hormuz 'at any price.'

outlets.name: NCRI (National Council of Resistance of Iran)
outlets.headline: Iran News in Brief: Economy bleak, IMF projects 6.1% GDP shrinkage, 68.9% inflation in 2026
outlets.summary: Iranian government economists paint 'bleak picture.' IMF: GDP -6.1%, inflation 68.9%, food inflation 105%. Araghchi in Doha for deal talks while domestic economic crisis deepens.

outlets.name: IRNA
outlets.headline: Iran delegation arrives Doha — Araghchi: 'Iran never wanted nuclear weapons'
outlets.summary: Iran's high-level Doha delegation: FM Araghchi, Speaker Ghalibaf, Central Bank Governor Hemmati. Araghchi admitted uranium 'deadlock' but declared 'Iran never wanted nuclear weapons.' Status: 'very far and very close' per Iranian FM spokesman.
